Politics.headline: Trump slaps up to 100% tariff on some brand-name drug imports in major America First push
Politics.detail: The Trump administration is implementing tariffs as high as 100% on certain brand-name drug imports as part of a significant America First initiative aimed at lowering drug costs.

Politics.headline: NY House GOP launches pressure campaign on Hochul to scrap climate law over soaring energy costs.
Politics.detail: New York House Republicans are initiating a campaign to pressure Governor Hochul to abandon the state's climate law, citing concerns over rapidly increasing energy costs.

Politics.headline: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
Politics.detail: Twenty-four states, led by Democrats, are suing the Trump administration to challenge new restrictions on mail-in voting procedures.

Politics.headline: Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request.
Politics.detail: President Trump is requesting a record $1.5 trillion in military spending as part of his budget proposal to Congress.

Politics.headline: 23 states sue Trump over new executive order targeting mail voting
Politics.detail: Officials from 23 states and the District of Columbia filed a lawsuit seeking to block President Trump's executive order that aims to restrict mail voting.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump touts unexpectedly high March jobs report as economy rebounds from weak February
Finance & Economy.detail: The March jobs report indicates a strong rebound with 178,000 new positions added, significantly exceeding forecasts, which Trump attributes to the positive impacts of tariffs.

Finance & Economy.headline: American workers' wage gains lost momentum in March despite strong hiring, economists say.
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite a robust hiring environment, wage growth for American workers slowed down in March, according to economists.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices are on the rise following Iran's denial of talks with the U.S., which reduces hopes for de-escalation in the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: US jobs market beats March expectations but February losses worse than first reported
Finance & Economy.detail: The US jobs market exceeded expectations in March; however, losses in February were more severe than initially reported.

Finance & Economy.headline: How the Middle East war is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada
Finance & Economy.detail: Experts are suggesting that the ongoing conflict in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz are contributing to rising mortgage rates in Canada.

Technology.headline: AI Can Now Prescribe You Psychiatric Medication in Utah
Technology.detail: A pilot program in Utah will use an AI chatbot from Doctronic to prescribe psychiatric medication, though there are limitations on what it can prescribe. This marks a significant, albeit cautious, step in integrating AI into mental healthcare.

Technology.headline: Artemis II Will Broadcast 4K High-Def Video from the Moon
Technology.detail: The Artemis II mission will utilize laser technology to provide high-speed communications, enabling the broadcast of 4K high-definition video from the Moon. NASA's Artemis II Optical Communications System (O2O) will allow for communication between the astronauts and ground crew at 260 megabits per second.

Technology.headline: Copilot Terms Claim Microsoft's AI Is for 'Entertainment Purposes Only'
Technology.detail: An update to Microsoft Copilot's terms of service states that the AI is for 'Entertainment Purposes Only'. This disclosure, similar to disclaimers used before psychic readings, has been noted by users on social media.

Technology.headline: Anthropic Rushes to Limit Leak of Claude Code Source Code
Technology.detail: Anthropic has attributed the inadvertent release of Claude Code's internal source code to a release packaging issue caused by human error, not a security breach. Approximately 1,900 files and 512,000 lines of code associated with the AI-powered coding assistant were compromised.

Technology.headline: AI machine sorts clothes faster than humans to boost textile recycling in China
Technology.detail: China is utilizing AI machines to sort clothes at a faster rate than humans, boosting textile recycling efforts. This technology is helping to improve the efficiency and scalability of textile recycling processes.

Science.headline: Binge Drinking Just Once a Month May Triple Your Risk of Liver Scarring
Science.detail: New research suggests that occasional binge drinking, even if infrequent, may be more dangerous than previously thought, potentially leading to significant liver damage.

Science.headline: Meteor Impacts May Have Sparked Life on Earth, Scientists Say
Science.detail: Asteroid impacts could have created hot, chemical-rich environments that were ideal for the emergence of early life on Earth, with impact-generated hydrothermal systems lasting long enough for life's building blocks to form.

Science.headline: Undergraduate Students Discover One of the Oldest Stars Ever
Science.detail: A group of undergraduate students stumbled upon a cosmic time capsule – one of the oldest stars ever discovered – while analyzing large astronomy datasets as part of a class project.

Science.headline: This Tiny Claw in a 500-Million-Year-Old Fossil Just Rewrote the Origin of Spiders
Science.detail: A newly examined fossil containing a tiny claw has provided new insights into the evolutionary origins of spiders, changing how scientists understand the early development of chelicerates.

Science.headline: Ancient Bees Found Nesting Inside Fossil Bones in Rare Cave Discovery
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered ancient bee nests inside fossil bones within a cave, offering a rare glimpse into the nesting habits of bees from the past.

Health.headline: Mission Hospital 'merry-go-round' sparks call for regulatory change
Health.detail: With four “Immediate Jeopardy” citations in five years, Asheville's Mission Hospital is on a “merry-go-round” of compliance enforcement, raising concerns about patient safety. The hospital has faced scrutiny for preventable patient deaths, unsafe patient transport, patient misidentification and harmful infection protocol.

Health.headline: Health officials see familiar flu trends this year
Health.detail: A regional health official in Southern Health-Santé Sud reports that this year's flu season is mirroring last year's trends, with an initial surge of influenza A subsiding as spring nears. Vaccination remains crucial, especially for high-risk groups like older adults, young children, and those with underlying health conditions.

Health.headline: Five deaths tied to health-care delays in Manitoba's latest critical incident reports
Health.detail: The latest critical incident reports in Manitoba reveal that five patients died due to delays in the healthcare system between April 1 and September 30, 2025. These deaths are part of 59 separate incidents during that period, highlighting the ongoing challenges of wait times and staffing shortages.

Health.headline: Vaping likely to cause lung and oral cancer, Australian researchers find in new review of evidence
Health.detail: A new review of evidence by Australian researchers suggests that vaping is likely to cause lung and oral cancer. This adds to the growing concerns about the health risks associated with e-cigarette use.

Health.headline: How a free pop-up health clinic in Texas is reaching a community of fishermen
Health.detail: A free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas, is providing healthcare to a community of Vietnamese fishermen amid rising healthcare costs and declining federal support. This initiative addresses the healthcare needs of a vulnerable population facing economic challenges.

Sports.headline: Oxford dominate Cambridge in women's race to end losing streak
Sports.detail: Oxford's women's team dominated the Cambridge crew in the annual Boat Race, putting an end to their losing streak in the competition. The focus of this year's race was on a fresh look, with Channel 4 taking over coverage from the BBC.

Sports.headline: Shreyas Iyer Breaches Code of Conduct Again, Entire PBKS Team Reprimanded
Sports.detail: Shreyas Iyer has breached the code of conduct, and the entire Punjab Kings team has been reprimanded, according to NDTV Sports. Details regarding the specific breach and reprimand were not immediately available.

Sports.headline: Forget this year's draft: Steelers and other teams already are jockeying for position for a loaded 2027 class
Sports.detail: Several NFL teams, including the Steelers, are already strategizing for the 2027 draft, anticipating a strong quarterback class. General manager Omar Khan has nine projected selections for the Steelers, and is trying to acquire more early-round picks.

Sports.headline: Dragons hit new low as Cowboys star
Sports.detail: Dragons coach Shane Flanagan is vowing to make changes after the Dragons were shut out 32-0 by North Queensland, slumping to the joint-venture's worst ever start to a season. Flanagan stated he is determined to find out why his club has fallen into an 0-5 hole.

Sports.headline: US sprinters Richardson and Coleman advance to the Stawell Gift semifinals in Australia
Sports.detail: American sprinters Richardson and Coleman have successfully advanced to the semi-finals of the Stawell Gift in Australia. This marks a significant achievement for both athletes as they compete in this renowned handicap race.

Entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar: The Revenge crosses Rs 1500 crore mark worldwide being the fastest Indian film to do so
Entertainment.detail: Dhurandhar 2 has crossed Rs 1500 crore mark worldwide, becoming the fastest Indian film to achieve this milestone, and has also emerged as the highest-grossing Indian film in the United States.

Entertainment.headline: Judge dismisses Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni
Entertainment.detail: A federal judge dismissed actor Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims in the legal battle between her and "It Ends With Us" director and her former co-star Justin Baldoni, but left intact a claim for retaliation.

Entertainment.headline: Ye attempts a comeback with sold-out LA-area concert, support from Lauryn Hill
Entertainment.detail: Kanye West, now known as Ye, performed a sold-out concert in Southern California with support from Lauryn Hill, marking a potential comeback after facing controversy for antisemitic remarks.

Entertainment.headline: Canadian Paul Alexander Nolan sinks teeth into Broadway's 'The Lost Boys' musical
Entertainment.detail: Paul Alexander Nolan stars as Max in Broadway's 'The Lost Boys: A New Musical,' which has gained the approval of Kiefer Sutherland and features flying sequences.

Entertainment.headline: From The Drama to Malcolm in the Middle: your complete entertainment guide to the week ahead
Entertainment.detail: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star in the new romcom 'The Drama', and the sitcom 'Malcolm in the Middle' is coming back with a revival.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2nd arrest made in Calgary investigation over South Asian extortions
Crime & Justice.detail: Calgary police say a traffic stop in the city's northeast led to a key arrest in an ongoing investigation into South Asian extortions involving shootings; the investigation is ongoing

Crime & Justice.headline: Criminal court statistics quarterly: January to March 2026
Crime & Justice.detail: The Ministry of Justice released the criminal court statistics for January to March 2026

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI makes arrest in hospice fraud scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI has arrested eight people in California connected to a $60 million health care fraud scheme.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man arrested at retirement home for wife's murder decades earlier
Crime & Justice.detail: Janice Randle was found dead in her bed in 1992, but police couldn't make an arrest in the case until new information emerged.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cleveland Armed Drug Dealer Sentenced to More Than 24 Years in Prison
Crime & Justice.detail: An armed drug dealer in Cleveland was sentenced to more than 24 years in prison.

Education.headline: Update on May 2026 examination session in the Middle East
Education.detail: The International Baccalaureate (IB) has released an update regarding the May 2026 examination session in the Middle East.

Education.headline: African Education Festival returns to South Africa in 2026, uniting educators under the theme of Ubuntu
Education.detail: The African Education Festival will return to South Africa in 2026, bringing educators together under the theme of Ubuntu.

Education.headline: Mumbai to host first IB Global Conference of 2026
Education.detail: Mumbai is set to host the first International Baccalaureate (IB) Global Conference of 2026.

Education.headline: Shaping our approach to AI as a community
Education.detail: The International Baccalaureate (IB) is working on shaping their approach to AI as a community.

Education.headline: Students feel 'misled' after being forced to pay back loans
Education.detail: Students are reporting feeling 'misled' after being forced to pay back loans.

Lifestyle.headline: 5 wardrobe pieces you can repeat every week with a fresh twist each time
Lifestyle.detail: Discover how to create versatile outfits by mixing and matching five essential wardrobe staples for a stylish and sustainable approach to fashion.

Lifestyle.headline: 5 simple ways to give your home a summer refresh without overspending
Lifestyle.detail: Learn easy and affordable tips to refresh your home for the summer season without breaking the bank, focusing on simple changes and cost-effective solutions.

Lifestyle.headline: Shoppers shelling out big bucks despite rising chocolate costs
Lifestyle.detail: Despite rising costs, consumers are still willing to spend money on chocolate, indicating a strong demand for the sweet treat.

Lifestyle.headline: United adds TSA wait times to app as DHS shutdown strains airport security lines
Lifestyle.detail: United Airlines is integrating TSA wait times into its app to help travelers navigate airport security more efficiently amidst strains caused by the DHS shutdown.

Lifestyle.headline: Artemis II update: Crew successfully completes critical translunar burn – Now racing toward the Moon
Lifestyle.detail: The Artemis II crew has successfully executed a vital translunar injection burn, propelling them on their journey to the Moon.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: U.S. cut aid to Africa. The continent proved resilient.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Trump administration's sharp reduction in aid to Africa in 2025 led to some pain, particularly in healthcare; however, many African nations displayed remarkable resilience, with sub-Saharan Africa's economy growing by 4.1 percent in 2025 and projected to grow by 4.3 percent this year. Despite the cuts, many African governments stepped up with long-needed reforms and are working to integrate African economies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Easter in the midst of war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This year, the celebration is marred by a US President who clearly believes he possesses the divine power to reshape the world as he sees fit, as the US-Israeli war on Iran is weaponized, especially through social media, as a religious conflict to stir fanaticism and radicalize the public. Both leaders use the Bible as a pretext to invade Iran, though we must also admit that Iran is not necessarily an innocent victim in this geopolitical struggle.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Maine's race to the top on taxes
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Maine's governor, Janet Mills, is supporting a millionaires tax for Maine as she runs against Graham Platner in the Senate primary. The two percent surtax would push Maine's top tax rate to 9.15 percent — exceeding nearby Massachusetts, which had the seventh highest top marginal individual income tax rate in the country in 2025, according to the Tax Foundation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Humanity's hopes ascended with Artemis II
Opinion/Editorial.detail: To watch Artemis soar into the skies this week was to get a glimpse of the hopes of humanity ascending, too. The astronauts who have been to the moon have seen our blue earth look "tiny, tiny against a black velvet background" of space.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The moral of Tony Gonzales' downfall is the lack of morality in our politics
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Tony Gonzales is out, Brandon Herrera is in, and Republicans are scrambling as the political ground shifts beneath their feet. The article discusses the lack of morality in politics.

World.headline: US jet downed over Iran; one crew reportedly rescued as search continues for second
World.detail: A US warplane has gone down over Iran and US forces have rescued one of the crew, major US media outlets reported Friday after Iranian media aired footage of aircraft wreckage; the search is ongoing for the second crew member.

World.headline: UAE says largest gas facility, Habshan, suffered 'significant damage' after air defence interception sparked fires.
World.detail: The UAE reports that its largest gas facility, Habshan, sustained considerable damage due to fires that ignited after an air defense interception.

World.headline: Second American fighter jet goes down by Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran war intensifies
World.detail: Amidst the ongoing US-Iran conflict, a second American fighter jet has been downed near the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in the region.

World.headline: Artemis II launches into orbit as NASA begins historic moon mission
World.detail: NASA's Artemis II mission has launched, sending astronauts on a journey around the moon and back, marking a significant step in renewed lunar exploration.

World.headline: Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group
World.detail: Iran has carried out the executions of two individuals who were convicted of being members of a banned opposition group.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZAPU leader warns against 'unscrupulous' bid to strip Zimbabweans of hard-won rights
Zimbabwe.detail: The ZAPU leader has cautioned against attempts to undermine the rights of Zimbabweans.

Zimbabwe.headline: Communities petition Parliament over destructive mining in Boterekwa, Shurugwi
Zimbabwe.detail: Communities are petitioning Parliament to address destructive mining practices in Boterekwa, Shurugwi.

Zimbabwe.headline: Excessive Bank Charges Set To Be Slashed
Zimbabwe.detail: Plans are underway to reduce the high bank charges in Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim seeks billions for energy infrastructure investments
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is seeking billions in investment for its energy infrastructure.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bulawayo water rationing remains in force
Zimbabwe.detail: Water rationing continues to be implemented in Bulawayo.
